verses.text: The LORD: send men to search the land of Canaan I give to Israel — one ruler of every tribe. Moses sent them from the wilderness of Paran by the LORD's commandment. Guidance: God invites Israel to scout the promise. Twelve spies — one per tribe. The look itself was meant to be a faith exercise, not a fact-checking expedition. How they would see it would decide their future.

How they would see it would decide their future.

The LORD: send men to search the land of Canaan I give to Israel — one ruler of every tribe. Moses sent them from the wilderness of Paran by the LORD's commandment. Names of the twelve: Reuben — Shammua. Simeon — Shaphat. Judah — Caleb. Issachar — Igal. Ephraim — Oshea (Moses called him Jehoshua). Benjamin — Palti. Zebulun — Gaddiel. Manasseh — Gaddi. Dan — Ammiel. Asher — Sethur. Naphtali — Nahbi. Gad — Geuel. Guidance: Twelve named men. Moses renames Oshea to Jehoshua — the LORD saves. Caleb and Joshua's names will become legend; the other ten will be remembered only as cautionary tales. The same trip, two different futures.

Moses sent: go up by the south, into the mountain. See the land and the people — strong or weak, few or many, in tents or strongholds, fat or lean, wood or none. Be of good courage; bring fruit of the land. (Time of firstripe grapes.) Guidance: Seven questions on Moses' list. He asks them to look hard. Be of good courage — already a hint that what they see may scare them. Faith looks twice and still trusts.

They went up, searched from Zin to Rehob. Came to Hebron — where Ahiman, Sheshai, Talmai, children of Anak, dwelt. Came to the brook of Eshcol; cut down a branch with one cluster of grapes — bare it between two on a staff — pomegranates and figs. Eshcol means cluster. Returned after forty days. Guidance: A cluster so big it takes two men to carry. The land delivers what God promised — sweet, heavy, abundant. The grapes alone should have ended the debate.

Came to Moses, Aaron, the congregation at Kadesh: we came to the land; it flows with milk and honey; this is its fruit. Nevertheless the people are strong, cities walled and great; we saw the Anakim. Amalekites in the south, Hittites, Amorites in the mountains, Canaanites by the sea. Guidance: Two true reports stitched together with one fatal word: nevertheless. Yes, it flows. Yes, it's ours. But — the giants are bigger than the promise. That's where their faith broke.

Came to Moses, Aaron, the congregation at Kadesh: we came to the land; it flows with milk and honey; this is its fruit. Nevertheless the people are strong, cities walled and great; we saw the Anakim. Amalekites in the south, Hittites, Amorites in the mountains, Canaanites by the sea. Guidance: Two true reports stitched together with one fatal word: nevertheless. Yes, it flows. Yes, it's ours. But — the giants are bigger than the promise. That's where their faith broke.

Caleb stilled the people: let us go up, possess it; we are well able. The men: we are not able; they are stronger than we. The land eats up its inhabitants; we saw the giants, sons of Anak — we were in our own sight as grasshoppers, and so in their sight. Guidance: Same fields, same giants — two reports. Caleb sees a land to be taken; the ten see themselves as bugs. The hardest enemy that day wasn't the Anakim — it was the mirror.
